dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7634 IH: Judicial Loyalty Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Stauber introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend title 28, United States Code, to provide for a limitation on the nationality of persons eligible to serve as judges of the United States.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Judicial Loyalty Act of 2026.

section.header: Limitation on eligibility to serve as a judge of the United States

subsection.text: Chapter 21 of title 28, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Limitation on nationality of eligible persons
section.text: No person shall be eligible to be appointed as a judge of the United States unless that person is a natural born citizen of the United States.

subsection.text: The table of sections for such chapter is amended by adding at the end the following:

toc.toc-entry: 464. Limitation on nationality of eligible persons.

section.text: In the case of any judge of the United States who is a United States citizen and who also is a citizen of a foreign country, if that judge does not officially renounce their foreign citizenship by not later than 60 days after the date of enactment of this Act, that judge may not continue in office.
